initiatives.name: Altadena Community Kitchen
initiatives.description: Providing fire relief efforts via donations and meals by partnering with neighbors of the Altadena community.

initiatives.name: Altadena Seed Library
initiatives.description: Altadena Seed Library is a growing network of seed exchange boxes, educational resources, and conservation initiatives. Through the distribution of free seeds and seed education, we are working to expand equitable access to shade and green spaces, increase food sovereignty, connect neighbors, and restore local ecosystems.

initiatives.name: Food Not Bombs DTLA
initiatives.description: We serve vegan food to the houseless every Saturday & some Wednesdays in DTLA. We also take donations such as clothing, blankets & toiletries to distribute to residents around Skid Row.

initiatives.description: Abolitionists fighting for food justice in LA. via food recovery, harm reduction, & mutual aid.

initiatives.name: LA Community Fridges
initiatives.description: Free food. Comida gratis. Take what you need, leave what you can. Agarra lo que necesitas, deja lo que no.

initiatives.name: Los Angeles Food Not Bombs
initiatives.description: Feeding the revolution w/ plant-based food.

initiatives.name: Polo's Pantry
initiatives.description: We bring dependable food access and culturally relevant nourishment to food insecure communities.

initiatives.name: Southeast LA Comida No Bombas

initiatives.description: Free organic produce box donations for Watts and South Central Los Angeles

initiatives.description: Volunteer-run free clinic from All Power Books.

initiatives.description: Bringing harm reduction services, needle exchange, and outreach to communities throughout Los Angeles since 1992.

initiatives.description: LA Abortion Support Collective (LAASC) is a volunteer led and run collective of trained abortion doulas based in the Los Angeles area.

initiatives.name: Aetna Street Solidarity
initiatives.description: Mutual aid group organizing around Aetna Street in Van Nuys.

initiatives.name: Echo Park Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: building bridges between housed & unhoused neighbors. you strong, i strong. you fed, i fed.

initiatives.name: Food Not Bulldozers
initiatives.description: We are a group of friends regularly doing supplies distro among unhoused communities in the South El Monte, “LA” area on Tongva land.

initiatives.description: An all-volunteer homeless outreach and advocacy organization in Koreatown, Los Angeles.

initiatives.name: LA CAN
initiatives.description: Fighting for Human Rights from Skid Row USA.

initiatives.name: LA Street Care & Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: Abolitionist mutual aid collective organizing in Los Feliz, Echo Park, Atwater, Historic Filipinotown, Silver Lake, and Rampart.

initiatives.name: LA Tenants Union
initiatives.description: The LA Tenants Union has local chapters across the city. Locals organize around neighborhood issues and help link neighborhood struggles to the larger movement for tenant power and housing justice.

initiatives.name: Los Angeles Community Action Network (LA CAN)
initiatives.description: The Los Angeles Community Action Network organizes people to fight back against oppression. BASED IN SKID ROW. WORKING GLOBALLY.

initiatives.name: Palms Unhoused Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: Abolitionists dedicated to community care in West LA —standing against state violence.

initiatives.name: Reclaiming Homes
initiatives.description: Impacted by the crisis, we are homeless & housing insecure families reclaiming vacant, publicly-owned homes to fight for housing as a human right.

initiatives.name: Street Watch LA
initiatives.description: Fighting to empower + protect the rights of poor/unhoused tenants across LA. Started by @dsa_la & @lacanetwork_official

initiatives.name: Chinatown Community for Equitable Development (CCED)
initiatives.description: Builds grassroots power with low-income and immigrant communities through organizing, education, and mutual help

initiatives.name: Selah Neighborhood Homeless Coalition
initiatives.description: Silver Lake, Echo Park, Los Feliz, Atwater Village, Hollywood in LA

initiatives.name: Fairfax Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: Fairfax Mutual Aid is a collective of local volunteers organizing material resources, care-based support and companionship to unhoused and housing insecure constituents in multiple districts in Los Angeles.

initiatives.description: We are queer, neurodivergent, anticapitalist, antifascist, abolitionist harm reductionists aiding over 100 people living without shelter in los angeles

initiatives.name: The Sidewalk Project
initiatives.description: We are an arts & peer empowerment program that exists to create wellness for those that live outdoor

initiatives.name: Trans Defense Fund LA
initiatives.description: Anticapitalist LA mutual aid project by an ally, self-defense resources for trans BIPOC, NOT a non-profit/501c3

initiatives.name:
Alliance of White Anti-Racists Everywhere - Los Angeles
                (AWARE-LA)
initiatives.description: We take collective action to build white anti-racist and multiracial alliances to contribute to a broad-based, social justice movement.

initiatives.name: SURJ Pasadena
initiatives.description: White people created white supremacy; it's our job to end it.

initiatives.name: White People 4 Black Lives
initiatives.description: a Los Angeles-based collective of white folks organizing for racial justice in solidarity with @blmlosangeles. Chapter of @showingupforracialjustice.

initiatives.name: Black Lives Matter Los Angeles
initiatives.description: Official #BlackLivesMatter Los Angeles chapter. Est. 7/13/13 to end state-sanctioned violence against Black people.

initiatives.name: Jail Support LA
initiatives.description: We're a grassroots collective dedicated to supporting anti-repression work in Los Angeles

initiatives.name: LA Anarchist Black Cross
initiatives.description: Los Angeles chapter of the Anarchist Black Cross Federation (Est. 1998). We support anarchist, anti-imperialist, & class war political prisoners.

initiatives.name: Coalition for Humane Immigrant Rights (CHIRLA)
initiatives.description: CA's largest immigrant rights organization. Founded in 1986.

initiatives.name: Stop LAPD Spying Coalition
initiatives.description: We are a community group working to build community power toward abolishing police surveillance.

initiatives.name: Garment Worker Center
initiatives.description: A worker center organizing with garment workers in Los Angeles to build power and fight exploitation in the garment industry. Through mutual aid, direct action, and worker-led organizing, they provide support services, leadership development, and advocate for better working conditions and fair wages.

initiatives.name: The Women's Protection and Resilience (WPV)
initiatives.description: A mutual aid organization providing support, resources, and community care to women in Los Angeles through direct assistance and solidarity networks.

initiatives.name: Whittier For Social Justice
initiatives.description: W4SJ is an autonomous radical collective committed to grassroots community support and social action in so-called Whittier, CA

initiatives.name: Comunidades Indígenas en Liderazgo (CIELO)
initiatives.description: An Indigenous women-led organization fostering mutual aid and solidarity through Indigenous community organizing in Los Angeles. They coordinate direct support, cultural preservation, and solidarity networks while building power through Indigenous leadership development and creating spaces for community care.

initiatives.name: Youth Justice Coalition
initiatives.description: A youth-led organization fostering mutual aid and community power through organizing against the criminalization of young people in Los Angeles. They coordinate direct support, political education, and solidarity networks while building power through youth leadership development, transformative justice, and creating spaces for system-impacted youth to lead movements for change.

initiatives.name: Centro Community Service Organization (Centro CSO)
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization fostering mutual aid and community power through Chicanx/Latinx organizing in Los Angeles. They coordinate education initiatives, direct action support, and solidarity networks while building power through community organizing, political education, and creating spaces for community empowerment and social justice.

initiatives.name: NOlympics LA
initiatives.description: Olympics always accelerate displacement & militarization. Cancel #LA28

initiatives.name: Community Economic Survival (CES)
initiatives.description: CES works to empower, educate and organize tenants living in private and government subsidized housing to fight to ensure tenants' rights and preserve quality and safe affordable housing

initiatives.name: CMB x Los Angeles
initiatives.description: Los Angeles branch of @communitymovementbuilders We are fighting for the total liberation and self-determination of all oppressed people

initiatives.name: Eagle and the Condor Liberation Front (ECLF)
initiatives.description: The Eagle and the Condor Liberation Front (ECLF) was envisioned at UCLA in October of 2017. Started primarily as a student organization, ECLF expanded beyond student politics to center the diverse Indigenous community of Los Angeles while working through the contours of the university. Since the organization's inception, the main focus of organizing has been conducted through political education, food sovereignty, internationalist solidarity, and public Indigenous speaker engagements.

initiatives.description: Fighting for Filipina women/LGBTQ+/GNC & children's rights & dismantling imperialism as part of the National Democratic movement. DM to join!

initiatives.description: SWOPLA is a peer support organization, run by sex workers and for sex workers.
